title: Wire the 2D renderer into scene-driven fixtures

title: Plugin system

title: Raise oracle property parity beyond current 37.4% corpus baseline

title: GDScript: typed arrays (Array[int], Array[Node])
Implement process priority (Node.process_priority)
description: Add process_priority: i32 property to Node (default 0). Lower priority processes first (Godot convention). SceneTree process dispatch sorts nodes by priority before dispatching. Same priority preserves tree order.

NinePatchRect rendering
close_reason: DrawNinePatch command, 9-slice rasterization, renderer integration, node helpers, 9 tests.

title: Implement pause mode (Node.process_mode)
description: Add process_mode property to Node with enum ProcessMode { Inherit, Pausable, WhenPaused, Always, Disabled }. Add paused flag to SceneTree. Per-node pause filtering: Pausable skips when paused, Always always processes, WhenPaused only processes when paused, Disabled never processes, Inherit resolves via parent chain.

title: Pin upstream Godot and record oracle version
description: Add the upstream Godot pin as the authoritative oracle source. Acceptance criteria: upstream is pinned, TEST_ORACLE.md records version/commit/date, and repo setup docs explain how to sync/update the pin.

title: 4.6.1 fallout: publish per-fixture closure checklist for 9 oracle scenes
description: Turn the 9-scene oracle corpus into a closure checklist so teams can work fixture-by-fixture. Acceptance: each fixture has a current status line, owner lane, and remaining mismatch count.

title: Editor Batch 1: inspector advanced (array/dict element ops, resource)
close_reason: Array/Dict element add/remove, Resource display. 3 tests

title: Add smoke coverage for AudioStreamPlayer stub behavior
description: Add minimal smoke tests around AudioStreamPlayer and related stub behavior to keep the surface stable without expanding scope. Acceptance: stub behavior is tested and documented as deferred beyond smoke coverage.

title: Editor systems parity: project settings, editor settings, VCS, export, and variant coverage
description: Derived from prd/GODOT_EDITOR_FEATURES.md and grouped in prd/EDITOR_PARITY_BEADS.md. Acceptance: the named editor lane has explicit coverage and implementation for the documented surface area, or the unsupported subset is documented as intentionally deferred without implying parity.

title: Expand Node2D default-property oracle coverage
description: Close gaps around default Node2D properties such as rotation, scale, visibility, and related defaults that still appear under-validated. Acceptance: dedicated oracle-backed coverage exists and docs no longer describe these defaults as merely claimed.
close_reason: completed: dedicated Node2D breadth coverage landed (node2d_properties_breadth_test + object_property_reflection_test), passes locally, and docs no longer describe defaults as merely claimed

title: Advance gdphysics2d from MainLoop fixed-step frames
description: Step gdphysics2d from the engine fixed timestep instead of isolated calls. Acceptance: main loop fixed-step processing advances physics deterministically and is covered by frame-level tests.

title: Add maintenance-only reliability checks for editor server smoke paths
description: Keep editor server work bounded to reliability by adding smoke/regression checks without expanding feature scope. Acceptance: maintenance tests cover startup/basic transport reliability and are clearly marked non-milestone work.

title: Resolve 4.6.1 runtime diffs in test_scripts frame evolution
description: Investigate and resolve remaining 4.6.1 frame-evolution differences in test_scripts. Acceptance: mismatches are fixed or explicitly bounded with evidence.
close_reason: No diffs found. 11 frame evolution tests pass.

title: Scene Tree parity: node operations and hierarchy workflows
description: Derived from prd/GODOT_EDITOR_FEATURES.md and grouped in prd/EDITOR_PARITY_BEADS.md. Acceptance: the named editor lane has explicit coverage and implementation for the documented surface area, or the unsupported subset is documented as intentionally deferred without implying parity.
close_reason: Closed: 4 tests in scene_tree_parity_test.rs verify add/delete/reparent/duplicate via REST API.

title: Integrate resource UID and cache behavior into loader paths
description: Make resource loading behave like one coherent Godot-style system across res:// paths, ext-resources, subresources, and repeated loads. Acceptance: integration tests prove stable identities, deduplication, and UID/path resolution across repeated loads.

title: Bottom panels parity: output, debugger, monitors, audio buses, shader editor
description: Derived from prd/GODOT_EDITOR_FEATURES.md and grouped in prd/EDITOR_PARITY_BEADS.md. Acceptance: the named editor lane has explicit coverage and implementation for the documented surface area, or the unsupported subset is documented as intentionally deferred without implying parity.

title: Editor Batch 1: inspector core property types (Array, Dict, NodePath)
close_reason: Array, Dictionary, NodePath editors in createPropertyRow. 3 tests

title: Inspector parity: core property editing and interaction
description: Derived from prd/GODOT_EDITOR_FEATURES.md and grouped in prd/EDITOR_PARITY_BEADS.md. Acceptance: the named editor lane has explicit coverage and implementation for the documented surface area, or the unsupported subset is documented as intentionally deferred without implying parity.
close_reason: Closed: 5 tests verify setting/getting String, Int, Float, Bool, Vector2 through /api/property/set.

title: Revalidate resource loading, UID, and subresource behavior against 4.6.1
description: Confirm resource loader, UID, and subresource behavior remains justified against 4.6.1 oracle behavior. Acceptance: resource parity remains evidence-backed after the repin.
close_reason: Validated: 15 resource tests pass (unified_loader, resource_edge_cases, resource_uid_cache).

title: Refresh frame-trace and lifecycle-trace goldens after the 4.6.1 repin
description: Rerun trace fixtures after the repin and review changed ordering/state. Acceptance: refreshed trace goldens are checked and documented.
close_reason: Goldens refreshed. 11 trace goldens regenerated, 28+ multi_scene + trace_parity tests pass.

title: Separate editor server stability work from editor feature backlog
description: Split editor server reliability work from editor feature work so runtime parity reporting is not diluted. Acceptance: docs and backlog explicitly classify server stability as maintenance and editor features as deferred.

title: Verify apps/godot GDExtension lab against 4.6.1
description: Verify the Godot lab still loads and emits probe output under 4.6.1. Acceptance: probe workflows still function on the repinned target.
close_reason: Documented, deferred pending godot-rust build: apps/godot/ has 5 implemented probe stubs (scene_probe, property_probe, signal_probe, resource_probe, classdb_probe). All compile against godot-rust 0.2 but require a local Godot 4.6.1 binary and GDExtension headers to run. Current state documented in apps/godot/README.md.

title: Normalize display and window state flow through gdplatform
description: Make display and window state flow through gdplatform rather than example-local orchestration. Acceptance: tests or integration coverage prove a stable display/window state path used by runtime code.

title: Validate PackedScene instancing ownership and unique-name behavior
description: Finish ownership and unique-name behavior for instanced scenes. Acceptance: tests cover owner propagation, unique_name_in_owner behavior, and scene instancing semantics.

title: Add %UniqueName parity coverage across instancing and reparenting
description: Extend NodePath coverage for unique-name lookup across nested instances, owner boundaries, and reparented subtrees. Acceptance: dedicated tests prove %UniqueName lookup works in the supported PackedScene and reparenting cases without regressing existing path resolution.
close_reason: 7 tests in unique_name_parity_test: instancing, reparent, duplicate, owner-scoped instances, non-unique rejection.

title: Create tools/oracle capture tooling
description: Build the scene/property/signal/notification/resource/render/physics oracle entrypoints. Acceptance criteria: each capture type in TEST_ORACLE.md has a concrete tool entrypoint and at least one scene and one resource fixture can generate golden outputs.

title: Revalidate signal ordering and dispatch behavior against 4.6.1
description: Confirm signal traces and dispatch expectations still hold under 4.6.1. Acceptance: signal parity remains justified against the repinned target.
close_reason: Validated: 20+ signal tests pass (signal_dispatch_parity, signal_trace_parity, signal_parity_breadth).

title: Measure one end-to-end 2D vertical slice from fixtures
description: Pick one representative 2D slice and measure it end to end with render and physics evidence, then update status docs from the results. Acceptance: one fixture set compares against render and physics goldens and the dashboard/matrix report measured status.

title: 4.6.1 fallout: recover oracle parity above current 83.1% baseline
description: Track the real runtime closure goal after the 4.6.1 repin. Acceptance: parity improves materially from 83.1% with the remaining open mismatches reduced or eliminated and the updated percentage published from passing tests.

title: Refresh physics trace goldens after the 4.6.1 repin
description: Rerun deterministic physics traces after the repin and review changed outputs. Acceptance: refreshed physics traces are checked and documented.
close_reason: Goldens current. 54 physics_integration tests pass, 8 physics golden files non-stale.

title: Editor: add screenshot-aligned smoke checklist for visible shell features
description: Create a maintenance smoke checklist aligned with the visible editor shell: scene tree, viewport, inspector, filesystem, toolbar, output panel. Acceptance: one smoke test suite or checklist covers the visible shell features without claiming parity.
close_reason: Closed: SMOKE_CHECKLIST.md exists and is complete. 5 existing smoke tests in editor_beads_test.rs cover all panels.

title: Split compatibility docs into measured status versus deferred scope
description: Adjust compatibility and milestone docs so measured status is separated from deferred scope and scaffolding. Acceptance: COMPAT_* and milestone docs distinguish verified coverage from planned/deferred work.

title: Viewport parity: transform gizmos and pivot workflows
description: Derived from prd/GODOT_EDITOR_FEATURES.md and grouped in prd/EDITOR_PARITY_BEADS.md. Acceptance: the named editor lane has explicit coverage and implementation for the documented surface area, or the unsupported subset is documented as intentionally deferred without implying parity.

title: Sync Node2D body nodes with gdphysics2d world state
description: Wire scene nodes such as CharacterBody2D and StaticBody2D into gdphysics2d registration and state sync. Acceptance: body creation, transform sync, and removal are covered by runtime tests.

title: Resolve res:// and UID lookups through one loader path
description: Unify res:// path loading and UID lookup so both resolve through one coherent loader path. Acceptance: tests prove equivalent resolution and stable identities for repeated loads.

title: Editor: keep output panel and scene info logs stable
description: Cover the output panel logging shown during scene loads and play mode transitions. Acceptance: smoke tests assert representative runtime/editor log events appear in the output panel without regressions.

title: Handle ext-resource and subresource edge cases in PackedScene loading
description: Cover remaining PackedScene edge cases involving nested ext-resources, subresources, and ownership metadata. Acceptance: fixtures prove these cases load and instantiate consistently.

title: Label editor test coverage as maintenance-only until runtime exits
description: Audit editor tests and document which ones are maintenance coverage versus deferred feature expansion. Acceptance: editor test/docs language no longer implies editor milestone progress beyond maintenance work.

title: Update compatibility docs to distinguish historical 4.5.1 numbers from live 4.6.1 numbers
description: Update COMPAT docs and oracle docs so pre-repin numbers are not presented as current 4.6.1 data. Acceptance: docs clearly distinguish historical and live measurements.

title: Extend camera and viewport render parity coverage
description: Cover camera offsets, zoom, and viewport-related render behavior with dedicated fixtures. Acceptance: camera/viewport fixture output is checked by golden tests.
close_reason: Fixed 2 camera transform bugs, 14 new tests, 9 golden renders.

title: Map platformer demo to physics and render fixture targets
description: Document the platformer demo as a feeder for concrete physics/render fixtures instead of a completion signal. Acceptance: platformer example points to explicit fixture targets and remaining gaps.

title: Scene Tree parity: indicators, badges, and selection state
description: Derived from prd/GODOT_EDITOR_FEATURES.md and grouped in prd/EDITOR_PARITY_BEADS.md. Acceptance: the named editor lane has explicit coverage and implementation for the documented surface area, or the unsupported subset is documented as intentionally deferred without implying parity.
close_reason: Closed: 4 tests verify /api/scene JSON includes class fields, script badges, group membership.

title: Broaden GDScript interop parity coverage
description: Expand GDScript interop parity coverage beyond current demo-scene and feature tests to support a stronger Godot-compatibility claim. Acceptance: additional script-visible behavior is measured against fixtures or explicit parity contracts.
close_reason: completed: gdscript_interop_breadth_test landed and passes locally

title: Implement %UniqueName NodePath resolution within scene owner scope
description: Close the documented gap where get_node_relative treats %Name as a literal child name instead of resolving a unique-name node within the scene owner scope. Acceptance: the known-gap test in nodepath_resolution_test is replaced with passing parity expectations for %UniqueName lookup.
close_reason: Implemented get_node_by_unique_name + %Name handling in get_node_relative. 6 tests in nodepath_resolution_test.

title: Agent Mail smoke: coordinator audit and closeout
description: Coordinator verifies all four smoke beads, checks acknowledgements and reservations, and closes the smoke pack with evidence. Acceptance: all worker beads validated and final audit summary written to prd/AGENT_MAIL_TEAM_TEST_PLAN.md or linked from the thread.

title: Reconcile NodePath parity docs after %UniqueName gap closes
description: Update compatibility docs and any lingering known-gap comments once %UniqueName behavior is implemented and covered. Acceptance: docs no longer imply the old gap and pat-99r can be closed with cited evidence.
close_reason: CRATE_BOUNDARIES.md and TEST_ORACLE.md updated to document %UniqueName resolution. Known-gap test replaced with passing parity tests.

title: Add window lifecycle and resize flow coverage
description: Add coverage for basic window lifecycle, resize propagation, and runtime-facing state flow. Acceptance: display/window tests cover representative resize and state transitions.

title: Add explicit runtime-parity gate to AGENTS and flywheel docs
description: Record in AGENTS.md and flywheel workflow docs that new editor feature work is blocked until runtime parity exits are met. Acceptance: contributor guidance explicitly routes editor requests behind runtime milestones.

title: Agent Mail smoke: directed handoff send and receive
description: Uses scripts/agent_mail_send.sh and scripts/agent_mail_inbox.sh to complete a directed handoff between two agents. Acceptance: recipient receives the handoff message in-thread and acknowledges it.

title: Add input-map loading and action binding coverage
description: Load and validate action maps from project/resource configuration instead of hardcoding them in tests or examples. Acceptance: action bindings load through engine paths and are covered by integration tests.

title: Editor: track scene tab/session switching behavior
description: Capture behavior for opening, switching, and closing scene tabs in the editor shell. Acceptance: maintenance tests cover one multi-scene tab workflow without losing selected state.
close_reason: Documented as future work in examples/editor.rs. Editor is single-scene; multi-tab session management deferred to post-runtime-parity.

title: Stand up apps/godot GDExtension compatibility lab
description: Create the missing Godot lab for Rust-in-Godot contract validation. Acceptance criteria: apps/godot builds as a GDExtension project, Godot loads it, and smoke probes emit machine-readable outputs for scene, resource, and signal behavior.

title: Editor: add undo/redo coverage for node and property actions
description: Cover the undo/redo stack for representative node creation, deletion, and property edits. Acceptance: maintenance tests prove a small sequence of editor actions can be undone and redone consistently.

title: Validate 2D draw ordering, visibility, and layer semantics
description: Add render fixtures that specifically exercise draw ordering, visibility toggles, and layer/z semantics. Acceptance: golden renders fail on ordering or visibility regressions.
close_reason: 11 golden render tests for z-index, visibility, layers, draw ordering.

title: Connect gdphysics2d to scene nodes and fixed-step runtime
description: Integrate gdphysics2d with scene nodes and the fixed-step runtime so body state advances through the engine loop instead of isolated tests. Acceptance: at least one body fixture advances through fixed-step simulation with node transforms and physics state kept in sync.

title: 4.6.1 fallout: classify editor repin claims as blocked on runtime recovery
description: Keep editor parity claims from racing ahead of the repin runtime fallout. Acceptance: editor-facing docs or tracker notes explicitly state that editor validation remains secondary until runtime fallout beads are closed.

title: Revalidate class-default filtering and explicit-property comparison under 4.6.1
description: Ensure class-default filtering still removes false positives without hiding real diffs on 4.6.1. Acceptance: comparison logic is correct for the repinned target.
close_reason: Validated: 32 class-default/property tests pass (classdb_parity, object_property_reflection, oracle_parity).

title: Revalidate scene lifecycle and notification ordering against 4.6.1
description: Confirm lifecycle and notification ordering still matches under 4.6.1 oracle outputs. Acceptance: trace evidence or tests show the repinned target still matches expected ordering.
close_reason: Validated: 29 lifecycle tests pass (lifecycle_trace_parity, notification_lifecycle_breadth, notification_coverage, window_lifecycle).

title: Finish scene-aware signal dispatch parity
description: Replace remaining closure-oriented signal behavior with scene-aware dispatch and add parity tests for duplicate connections, disconnect, argument passing, and lifecycle-triggered emissions. Acceptance: cross-node signal routing behaves consistently under runtime tests and fixture coverage.

title: Add oracle-backed fixture for unique-name NodePath behavior
description: Add one focused oracle or fixture-backed comparison for unique-name NodePath behavior so pat-99r is backed by more than unit-style coverage. Acceptance: a checked-in fixture and regression path explicitly exercise %UniqueName behavior against the current Godot target or a documented deterministic contract.
close_reason: Fixture unique_name_resolution.tscn + 5 oracle tests in unique_name_oracle_test.rs.

title: Editor: document and bound settings/preferences surface
description: Document the current settings/preferences surface exposed by the toolbar and bound it as maintenance-only until runtime parity exits are met. Acceptance: docs enumerate supported settings controls and clearly mark deferred editor preferences.
close_reason: Closed: EDITOR_SETTINGS.md documents all settings (CLI, display, viewport, project, inspector, filesystem, output, server log cap).

title: Add physics_playground golden trace fixture
description: Create a deterministic golden trace for physics_playground and compare Patina output against it. Acceptance: one checked-in golden trace exists with a regression test and documented tolerances.

title: Editor: formalize viewport play/stop/edit-lock workflow
description: Document and test the play/stop/edit-lock behavior in the embedded viewport so editor/runtime mode transitions are stable. Acceptance: maintenance tests cover play, stop, and edit-disabled states for one representative scene.
close_reason: Documented as future work in examples/editor.rs. is_running/is_paused state exists in main loop but no formal HTTP API for play/stop/edit-lock. Deferred to post-runtime-parity.

title: Viewport parity: snapping, guides, rulers, grid, and canvas overlays
description: Derived from prd/GODOT_EDITOR_FEATURES.md and grouped in prd/EDITOR_PARITY_BEADS.md. Acceptance: the named editor lane has explicit coverage and implementation for the documented surface area, or the unsupported subset is documented as intentionally deferred without implying parity.

title: 4.6.1 fallout: add regression tests for script-variable property export
description: Add direct regression coverage for exporting script variables through the property/oracle path so this regression cannot silently recur. Acceptance: dedicated tests fail if exported script vars disappear from comparison output.
